pattern: Precomputing a 2D prefix-sum table once lets any axis-aligned rectangle sum be answered with a fixed four-term inclusion-exclusion formula in constant time.
hints: Answering each query by walking the rectangle is too slow when there are many queries; do the heavy work once up front.
Build a table where each cell holds the sum of everything above-and-left of it, including itself.
The sum of a rectangle is the big prefix block minus the strip above minus the strip to the left, plus the corner that was subtracted twice (inclusion-exclusion).

edge_inputs.description: the statement's own matrix with three overlapping sub-rectangles
single-cell matrix queried at its only cell
whole-matrix region equals the total of all cells
single row and single column regions touch the prefix edges
negative values across the rectangle exercise the inclusion-exclusion signs

stress.description: random matrix then many random valid rectangle queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, 40)
    cols = rng.randint(1, 40)
    matrix = [[rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
    ops = ['NumMatrix']
    args = [[matrix]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        r1 = rng.randint(0, rows - 1)
        r2 = rng.randint(r1, rows - 1)
        c1 = rng.randint(0, cols - 1)
        c2 = rng.randint(c1, cols - 1)
        ops.append('sumRegion'); args.append([r1, c1, r2, c2])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: queries are constructed with r1<=r2 and c1<=c2 so every rectangle is valid
